description: A join to a finer-grained table multiplies fact rows and silently double-counts measures.
body: Failure mode: a fact table is joined to a dimension or bridge table at a finer grain than expected, so each fact row fans out into several and additive measures are summed multiple times, inflating totals. Detection signals: a measure total that exceeds an independent source, and a row count after the join larger than the fact table alone. Do not wrap the result in DISTINCT to hide the inflation. Safer fallback: re-establish the intended grain, aggregate the finer table to the fact grain before joining or use a properly modeled bridge, and verify the post-join row count and measure totals match the single-grain fact table.
